findings.blocker: Two genuine missing-Mathlib bridges block honest closure: GAP G1 (quasicoherent + finite-type ⇒ finite section module over affine: ∀ {W}, IsAffineOpen W → Module.Finite Γ(X,W) Γ(F,W)) and GAP G3 (flat-locality assembly from a finite affine source cover). G1 blocks even constructing the witness V.

attempts.strategy: Feasibility audit of the stated theorem BEFORE proving
attempts.insight: FOUND A CORRECTNESS BUG: theorem was FALSE as stated. [LocallyOfFiniteType p] does NOT entail QuasiCompact in Mathlib. Counterexample: S=Spec ℤ, X=⊔_i Spec ℤ (infinite disjoint union), p=fold map, F = ℤ/pᵢℤ on component i — no non-empty open flat locus exists. The blueprint's 'locally of finite type ⟹ quasi-compact' was the underlying error.

attempts.strategy: Add [QuasiCompact p] to the signature (matches Nitsure §4 'finite type morphism'), then discharge the algebraic-input instances
attempts.code_tried: theorem genericFlatness ... (p : X ⟶ S) [LocallyOfFiniteType p] [QuasiCompact p] ...; haveI : IsDomain Γ(S,U₀) := @IsIntegral.component_integral S _ U₀ ⟨⟨x₀, hx₀⟩⟩; haveI : IsNoetherianRing Γ(S,U₀) := IsLocallyNoetherian.component_noetherian ⟨U₀, hU₀aff⟩
attempts.insight: Build green, 1 intended sorry @2264. component_integral wants [Nonempty ↥↑U] whose coercion does NOT match ↥U₀ nor ↥(U₀:Set ↥S) at instance reducibility — supply via @-application. genericFlatness not protected, no downstream consumers, safe to re-sign.
